images.caption: Figure 1:Main challenges for 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR points.First, due to spatial ambiguity issue of 3D human and object points, previous SOTA method[an2025pre]fails to predict accurate pose. Second, class imbalance issue occurs in frequently interacting body parts (↓\downarrow) such as hand and foot, where the number of points are severely low compared to other body parts, object, and background.

Figure 2:Overall pipeline of HOIL.Given an input point cloud𝐏\mathbf{P}, we first embed it into input features𝐅p\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{p}}and encode them using a multi-stage encoder with CPPool. The encoded features are then progressively decoded through a multi-stage decoder to produce the final decoder features𝐅p,dec(0)\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{p},\mathrm{dec}}^{(0)}. At each decoding stage, keypoint queries𝐐\mathbf{Q}are iteratively updated via multi-stage keypoint decoder with decoder features. Lastly, HOIL predicts point-level segmentation𝐒p\mathbf{S}_{\mathrm{p}}and contact𝐂p\mathbf{C}_{\mathrm{p}}from𝐅p,dec(0)\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{p},\mathrm{dec}}^{(0)}, and 3D keypoint coordinates𝐊\mathbf{K}and keypoint-level contact𝐂K\mathbf{C}_{\mathrm{K}}from keypoint queries𝐐\mathbf{Q}.

Figure 3:Human-object interaction-aware contrastive learning.C and NC refer to contact and non-contact, respectively. Others represent other human body parts except hand and foot. For the simplicity of illustration, we do not divide hand and foot for C and NC whereas our HOICL does operate on those cases as well.

Figure 4:Qualitative comparison of 3D human pose estimation with the state-of-the-art method[an2025pre]on Waymo[sun2020scalability].Red circles indicate exemplar regions that HOIL outperforms previous methods.

header: S2Details of keypoint and part definition

images.caption: Figure S1:Correlation between segmentation accuracy and human pose estimation error of state-of-the-art method[an2025pre]on Waymo[sun2020scalability].

Figure S2:Error by human body joints of state-of-the-art method[an2025pre]on Waymo[sun2020scalability]and InterCap[huang2024intercap].Red rectangles indicate errors from wrists and ankles which correspond to frequently interacting parts (e.g.,hands and feet).

Figure S3:Qualitative comparison of 3D human pose estimation with the state-of-the-art method[an2025pre]on Waymo[sun2020scalability].We assort samples where the SOTA method has highest errors from the dataset. Red circles indicate exemplar regions that HOIL outperforms previous methods.

Figure S4:Qualitative comparison of 3D human pose estimation with the state-of-the-art method[an2025pre]on SLOPER4D[dai2023sloper4d].We assort samples where the SOTA method has highest errors from the dataset. Red circles indicate exemplar regions that HOIL outperforms previous methods.
